_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Allocate collection assets based on leadership's guidance, priorities, and/or operational emphasis.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to make strategic resource allocation decisions under uncertainty, balancing proven approaches with experimental initiatives and adjusting course as evidence emerges.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI LS domain. LS-A-003 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RM-K-011 (RM, cosine: 0.450), LS-T-007 (LS, cosine: 0.446).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.6/10 (raw 0.259). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [allocate, assets, based, collection, emphasis, guidance] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, adjusting, allocation, approaches, balancing, course] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.861 (P=0.866, R=0.857). Balanced P/R — the 11-word DCWF element and 21-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (93.0%). "Allocate collection assets based on leadership's guidance, priorities, and/or op" and "Ability to make strategic resource allocation decisions under uncertainty, balan" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.460 > STS 0.259 (gap +0.200). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 3.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [allocate, assets, based, collection, emphasis, guidance]. WIDAI terms: [ability, adjusting, allocation, approaches, balancing, course].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.200 with low STS 0.259. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
